---
title: "ED Price Transparency Tool"
subtitle: "Making CMS hospital pricing data accessible to patients"
author: "GLHLTH 562 · Duke University"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    highlight-style: github
    code-line-numbers: false
    footer: "GLHLTH 562 · ED Price Transparency · Hillsborough County, FL"
    logo: ""
    width: 1280
    height: 720
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## The problem {background-color="#0F6E56"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Emergency care is the most opaque healthcare purchase in America

A chest pain workup in Hillsborough County can cost

::: {.fragment}
**$3,060** at one hospital
:::
::: {.fragment}
**$4,390** at another — for identical services
:::
::: {.fragment .fade-in}
<br>
Patients have no way to know this before they go.
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```
Tampa General    $4,118
St. Joseph's     $3,640
AdventHealth     $4,390  ← highest
HCA Brandon      $3,520
HCA S. Tampa     $3,710
HCA W. Tampa     $3,540
S. FL Baptist    $3,060  ← lowest
```
*Cash prices for a chest pain workup*
:::
:::
:::

---

## The data exists — but it's unusable {background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Since 2021**, CMS requires all hospitals to publish machine-readable files (MRFs) of every charge

- Up to **2 GB** per file
- Cryptic billing codes: `CPT 84484`
- No plain-language descriptions
- No way to compare across hospitals
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
### The gap we're bridging

```
Patient types:
  "chest pain"
       ↓
Claude API maps to:
  CPT 99285, CPT 93010,
  CPT 84484, DRG 313
       ↓
App looks up prices in
  7 hospital MRFs
       ↓
Patient sees ranked list
  with real prices
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## What we built {background-color="#065A82"}

::: {.r-fit-text}
An interactive R Shiny app that turns a symptom into a price comparison
:::

::: {.columns style="margin-top: 1rem;"}
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.fragment}
**🔍 Search**

Free-text symptom input with Claude API billing code mapping
:::
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.fragment}
**🗺️ Map**

Interactive Leaflet map of all 7 Hillsborough County EDs
:::
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.fragment}
**📊 Compare**

Dot plots and treemaps showing price breakdowns
:::
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.fragment}
**🧮 Calculator**

Out-of-pocket estimator with real insurance inputs
:::
:::
:::

---

## Data pipeline {background-color="#1a1a2e"}

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[User types symptom] --> B{find_match}
    B -->|Known condition| C[Local MRF data]
    B -->|Unknown| D[Claude API\nclaude-sonnet-4]
    D --> E[Returns CPT/DRG\ncodes as JSON]
    E --> C
    C --> F[Render results\nSearch · Map · Charts · Calculator]
    
    G[GitHub Actions\nevery Monday] --> H[fetch_mrf_data.R]
    H --> I[Download 7 hospital MRFs\nup to 2GB each]
    I --> J[Filter to ED codes\nCPT · HCPCS · DRG]
    J --> K[hillsborough_ed_prices.json]
    K --> C

    style A fill:#1D9E75,color:#fff
    style D fill:#534AB7,color:#fff
    style G fill:#D85A30,color:#fff
    style F fill:#065A82,color:#fff
```

---

## Three capabilities beyond user input

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
### 🔌 API integration

- CMS MRFs fetched programmatically at runtime
- Anthropic Claude API called at query time
- No manual CSV downloads
- Hospitals legally prohibited from blocking automated access

*45 CFR Part 180*
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
### 🤖 GenAI in pipeline

- Claude performs **real clinical classification**
- Symptom → CPT/HCPCS/DRG mapping
- Structured JSON output
- Degrades gracefully when API unavailable

*Not cosmetic — the app cannot function without it*
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
### ⚙️ Automation

- GitHub Actions workflow
- Runs **every Monday 6 AM UTC**
- Re-downloads all 7 hospital MRFs
- Commits updated prices to repo
- Uploads processing log as artifact

*.github/workflows/mrf-refresh.yml*
:::
:::

---

## Key R packages

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

| Package | Role |
|---------|------|
| `shiny` | Core app framework |
| `bslib` | Bootstrap 5 UI components |
| `httr2` | Claude API + MRF HTTP calls |
| `jsonlite` | JSON parsing |
| `leaflet` | Interactive hospital map |
| `plotly` | Interactive charts |

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

| Package | Role |
|---------|------|
| `ggplot2` | Chart construction |
| `reactable` | Heatmap + OOP table |
| `dplyr` | Data manipulation |
| `stringr` | Symptom matching |
| `readr` | Large MRF CSV reading |
| `logger` | Pipeline logging |

:::
:::

---

## Feature: ZIP code + distance filter

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**The problem:** All 7 hospitals always show equally — but a patient in Brandon has no reason to consider South Tampa hospitals first.

**The solution:**

1. User enters a ZIP code
2. App looks up coordinates from a Hillsborough County ZIP table
3. **Haversine formula** calculates straight-line distance to each hospital
4. A slider filters out hospitals beyond a chosen radius

```r
haversine_distance <- function(lat1, lng1, lat2, lng2) {
  R    <- 3958.8  # Earth radius in miles
  phi1 <- lat1 * pi / 180
  phi2 <- lat2 * pi / 180
  dphi <- (lat2 - lat1) * pi / 180
  dlng <- (lng2 - lng1) * pi / 180
  a    <- sin(dphi/2)^2 +
          cos(phi1) * cos(phi2) * sin(dlng/2)^2
  2 * R * asin(sqrt(a))
}
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
**Result:** Entering ZIP `33511` (Brandon) and filtering to 15 miles automatically surfaces the two nearest hospitals — no manual sorting needed.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Feature: Insurance payer filter

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**The problem:** "Cash price" is only useful for the uninsured. Most patients have insurance — but what does *their* plan actually pay?

**The solution:**
- Dropdown with 8 Florida insurers: Florida Blue, Aetna, UnitedHealthcare, Cigna, Humana, Medicaid, Medicare
- `get_payer_price()` applies payer-specific multipliers derived from de-identified MRF negotiated rate ranges
- Prices update instantly across all cards, value boxes, and charts

```r
get_payer_price <- function(base_cash, hosp_id, payer_id) {
  if (payer_id == "cash") return(base_cash)
  multiplier <- PAYER_MULTIPLIERS[[hosp_id]][[payer_id]]
  round(base_cash * multiplier)
}
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
**What you see:**

Medicare rates are **30-35% below** cash prices

UnitedHealthcare rates are **45-55% above** cash prices

The difference in what insurers pay at Tampa General for a chest pain workup:

- Medicare: ~$2,800
- Cash: $4,118
- UnitedHealthcare: ~$6,200
:::
:::
:::

---

## Feature: Out-of-pocket calculator

**The most actionable feature — estimates what a patient actually pays**

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
**Inputs:**

- Insurance plan selection
- Annual deductible ($)
- Amount already met toward deductible ($)
- Coinsurance percentage after deductible (%)
- Out-of-pocket maximum ($)
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}
**The calculation:**

```r
deduct_remaining <- max(0, deductible - deductible_met)

# Step 1: apply remaining deductible
deduct_applied <- min(base_price, deduct_remaining)
after_deduct   <- base_price - deduct_applied

# Step 2: apply coinsurance to remainder
coins_applied  <- after_deduct * coinsurance

# Step 3: cap at out-of-pocket maximum
total_oop  <- deduct_applied + coins_applied
oop_spent  <- deductible_met * coinsurance
final_oop  <- min(total_oop, max(0, oop_max - oop_spent))
```
:::
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Example:** $3,000 deductible, $500 met, 20% coinsurance, $6,000 OOP max → chest pain at Tampa General costs you **$2,963** not $4,118
:::

---

## Feature: Treatment definitions

**The problem:** Patients see `CPT 84484 — Troponin I` and have no idea what it means or why they're being charged for it.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Every billing code badge and service line item now has a hover tooltip with three parts:**

1. **What it is** — full name and CPT code
2. **What it does** — plain-language description
3. **Why you see this** — why it appears on an ED bill specifically
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
**Example: Troponin I**

> *Measures a protein released into the bloodstream when heart muscle cells are damaged. Elevated levels strongly indicate a heart attack. Results typically return in 1–2 hours and are often repeated after 3–6 hours.*
>
> **Why you see this:** The definitive blood test for ruling a heart attack in or out. Cannot be skipped for a serious chest pain workup — you may be billed for it twice if a repeat draw is ordered.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Design decisions and trade-offs

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**What we chose and why:**

- **Static price data + live Claude API** — avoids 2GB downloads on every search while still enabling unknown symptom mapping
- **Payer multipliers instead of raw MRF payer data** — full MRF payer extraction requires parsing hundreds of plan name variations per hospital; multipliers give clinically reasonable estimates immediately
- **Haversine over Google Maps API** — no API key required, accurate enough for within-county comparisons
- **Quarto for presentation, Shiny for product** — right tool for each job
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**What we would improve with more time:**

- Parse actual payer-specific negotiated rates from MRFs by plan name
- Add real-time wait time data from hospital websites
- Geocode user address (not just ZIP centroid) for more accurate distances
- Add urgent care comparison — many conditions are cheaper at urgent care
- Deploy to shinyapps.io for persistent public access
- Add a shareable URL encoding current search state
:::
:::

---

## What this solves

::: {.r-fit-text}
The average American cannot answer this question:
:::

> *"If I go to the nearest ER with chest pain, how much will I pay?"*

::: {.fragment}
**Before this tool:** 7 separate hospital websites, each with a 2GB CSV file full of billing codes, no way to compare.
:::

::: {.fragment}
**After this tool:** Type "chest pain", select your insurer, enter your ZIP. See ranked prices in under 5 seconds.
:::

::: {.fragment style="margin-top: 2rem; text-align: center;"}
The data was always public. We made it usable.
:::

---

## {background-color="#0F6E56"}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Thank you
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; color: white; margin-top: 2rem;"}
**ED Price Transparency Tool — Hillsborough County, FL**

GLHLTH 562: Data Science and Visualization with R

Duke University
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); margin-top: 1.5rem; font-size: 0.9rem;"}
Built with R · Shiny · httr2 · leaflet · plotly · Anthropic Claude API

CMS Hospital Price Transparency data (45 CFR Part 180)
:::
